title: What it takes to build *realtime* voice models | Voxtral
summary: Julia Turc examines Mistral's Voxtral Realtime and Voxtral TTS as examples of modern realtime voice model architecture. The video covers modular vs end-to-end speech design, audio tokenization techniques including vector quantization and finite scalar quantization, and key historical milestones like Whisper, Whisper Streaming, and WaveNet.
writeup: Julia Turc breaks down the architecture behind realtime voice models, using Mistral's Voxtral Realtime and Voxtral TTS as the running examples. The video contrasts modular pipelines with end-to-end speech design and explains audio tokenization methods including vector quantization and finite scalar quantization, then traces historical milestones from WaveNet through Whisper and Whisper Streaming. It connects these techniques to concepts like residual vector quantization, acoustic tokens, and delayed streams modeling. The result clarifies how audio gets turned into tokens and how low-latency speech systems are assembled.
